calendar.matters:
Petition To Remove Trustee For Breach Of Trust; To Compel Trustee To Account; And To Appoint Succesor Trustee
Off Calendar Aug-28-2020 Off Calendar

Petition To Remove Trustee For Breach Of Trust; To Compel Trustee To Account; And To Appoint Succesor Trustee
Off Calendar Jun-30-2020 Continued To Sep-01-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Judy Hitchcock appeared by telephone for Kaye Hoxter (Petitioner/Beneficiary).
David Myers appeared by telephone for Platte River Insurance Company.
Mary Pynenburg appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that at the last hearing before Judge Busch there was a venue issue. Ms. Hitchcock stated that she is hopeful to resolve the issues and have a stipulated agreement without having to file in Alameda County, and that her understanding is that Ms. Pynenburg is willing to resign, but they have other issues to resolve. Ms. Pynenburg stated that she really needs more time to resolve this matter and requested a 60-day continuance. The Court indicated that it would not continue the matter without a commitment from Ms. Pynenburg. Ms. Pynenburg stated that she commits to make it a priority to try and resolve the matter. The Petition to Remove Trustee for Breach of Trust, etc. is continued to September 1, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed the Parties that it is not anticipating any further extensions.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Reyes. Not reported.

Petition To Remove Trustee For Breach Of Trust; To Compel Trustee To Account; And To Appoint Succesor Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Jun-30-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Remove Trustee For Breach Of Trust; To Compel Trustee To Account; And To Appoint Succesor Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-03-2020 Continued To Apr-07-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Judy Hitchcock for and with Petitioner, Kaye Hoxter.
Jodi Bando present in courtroom.
Mary Pynenburg, Respondent, in propria persona via Court Call.
Shashauna Szczechowicz, of Wolkin Curran LLP for Platte River Insurance via Court Call.

The Court noted the Trustee is in Alameda County and whether the trust case belongs there. Ms. Hitchcock stated there have been added development as of yesterday, stating that Ms. Pynenburg requested a continuance, that if Ms. Pynenburg resigns and Jodi Bando who resides in San Francisco takes over as Trustee the case can remain here. The Court noted that as of now, the case belongs with Alameda County and Alameda County should take care of the issue of removing the Trustee. Ms. Hitchcock inquired if she could file an ex parte, or if the court can order trustee to account and request replacement of trustee, and further stated that this case arose out of a San Francisco Conservatorship, that Ms. Hoxter is disabled and could not get to Alameda. The Court noted that it cannot rule in advance for an ex parte, that it still technically faces the same venue issue, and will continue the matter for Ms. Hitchcock to either come up with other argument or try to come up with a different way to do this.

The Petition to Remove Trustee for Breach of Trust, etc., is continued to April 7, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.
